Patch Set 1:

consider the following case: rm a; mkdir a; where "rm" is local while "mkdir" is distributed. don't we need to guarantee "rm" before "mkdir" is executed?
probably we should discuss this online, I'm not sure on the approach we took.

Patch Set 1:

yes, indeed, I'll think about it.

Patch Set 3:

Lai, could you please rebase the patch?

Patch Set 3:

(3 comments)

Patch Set 3:

"Lai, but the lock taken for local unlink still disappears once ACK'ed by the client?"

Alex, not sure I follow the question correctly. You mean the conflict between create and unlink? or You mean we should not release lock until the commit?

Patch Set 3:

yes, a distributed operation can depend on a local operation. if the local operation isn't commited (but already ACK'ed by the client), then there is no LDLM representing this unstable state. so no lock conflict with the distributed operation, no sync and then we depend on the client to provide with the replay which isn't always the case?

Patch Set 3:

There are two rules

1. If the resource is being hold by a local lock, and it conflicts with the lock request from another MDT, then it should commit the changes, then release the local lock.
2. If the resource is being hold by a cross-MDT lock, and conflicts with local lock request, then it should also commit the changes, then release the lock.

I think 2 has been done in http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/12530/. Alex: you mean 1 here? right?

Patch Set 3:

I think for 1, the easiest way would be

a) enable COS by default, so all of locks will be downgrade to COS lock after request handler.

b) and in mdt_blocking_ast, for COS lock, if the conflict lock from another MDT, then we will do synchronization anyway. No matter if it is from the same client or not.

Patch Set 3:

hi Alex, in my understanding once COS is enabled, REP-ACK won't take effect, so for unlink after reply is acknowledged the local COS lock still stays there, until transaction is committed.

Patch Set 3:

(3 comments)

Patch Set 4:

Alex, sorry for the late reply, I just get back from vacation.

The only way to enable CoS on DNE environment that I once thought of is to add an ioctl in mdo_iocontrol to check OSP device count in LOD layer, and cache the result in MDT layer. Any suggestion for this?

Patch Set 4:

probably exact mechanism isn't that important at the moment - we can consider few options. the more important are two things:
1) have it enabled as otherwise all this doesn't work?
2) benchmark the overhead

Patch Set 4:

ok, I'll do it soon.

Lai, could you please create a ticket, I will ask around if some one can help. Thanks.

Patch Set 6:

first of all, I'm not sure what do you mean by "multiple slot RPC", can you please explain?
as for multiple creations within a single directory.. I'd think that they should cause mkdir creation and do not collide each other as their locks don't collide?
one thing I'm still concerned is that to make the schema working we need to retain all PW/CW/EX LDLM locks until corresponding update is committed. with 10K creates/sec this would give as ~50K locks by commit point. at the moment I'm not sure how big performance impact will be.

Patch Set 6:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

"first of all, I'm not sure what do you mean by "multiple slot RPC", can you please explain? as for multiple creations within a single directory.. I'd think that they should cause mkdir creation and do not collide each other as their locks don't collide? "

It is my understanding that once multiple-slot lastrcvd is supported, client will send multiple create RPC (under same directory), so it will cause locks collides. (why not?), and once lock collide happens, strict COS will cause synchronization.  


"one thing I'm still concerned is that to make the schema working we need to retain all PW/CW/EX LDLM locks until corresponding update is committed. "

exactly, but for share directory creation (no cross-MDT operation), this should not happen at all. But with the current patch, I am not sure about that.

parents.subject: LU-3538 cos: CoS can take effect for cross-MDT op only

commit.subject: LU-3538 dne: eliminate dependency in striped dir creation
commit.message:
LU-3538 dne: eliminate dependency in striped dir creation

DNE needs to eliminate dependency between 'mkdir a/b' and any
operation on 'a' because 'b' may be a striped directory, which is
able to recover from update log, if 'a' can't be recovered, it
will cause problem.

To achieve this,  mkdir needs to save child lock, so that
subsequent mkdir under it can know the conflict.

Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Change-Id: I1887f7c7978d94695563cf77290148b9c35170c7
